context: Tests whether the agent uses the canonical test_command and typecheck_command from docs/agents/commands.md rather than inventing commands from package.json or using standard runner names directly. Also tests TDD discipline: vertical slicing and behavior-oriented tests.

checklist.name: Uses canonical test_command
checklist.description: tdd-log.md shows test runs using 'npm run test:unit' (the test_command from commands.md), not 'npm test', 'jest', 'npx jest', 'yarn test', or any other form

checklist.name: Does not invent test command
checklist.description: Does NOT use npm test, jest, npx jest, yarn test, or any test command not found in docs/agents/commands.md

checklist.name: Uses canonical typecheck_command
checklist.description: tdd-log.md shows type-checking using 'npm run typecheck' (the typecheck_command from commands.md), not 'tsc', 'npx tsc', or any other form

checklist.name: Does not invent typecheck command
checklist.description: Does NOT use tsc, npx tsc, or any typecheck command not from commands.md

checklist.name: Vertical slices evidenced
checklist.description: tdd-log.md shows tests added one at a time with implementation following each — at least 3 distinct RED→GREEN cycles logged

checklist.name: Behavior-describing test names
checklist.description: Test names describe user-observable behavior (e.g. 'user sees unread count decrease after marking read') not implementation

checklist.name: Public interface tests
checklist.description: Tests only invoke public methods of NotificationService — no access to internal storage, private methods, or internal state

checklist.name: One assertion per test
checklist.description: Each test function contains one logical assertion (one expect statement or one conceptual outcome)

checklist.name: Minimal implementation per cycle
checklist.description: tdd-log.md shows implementation was limited to what was needed to pass each test, not written in bulk ahead of tests

checklist.name: Tracer bullet first
checklist.description: tdd-log.md shows the first cycle is the simplest possible test (e.g. 'can create a notification') before more complex scenarios
